Models.Name: Source Credibility Model
Models.Description: Evaluates the reliability and trustworthiness of anonymous and named sources providing information for print newspaper investigations. Incorporates document provenance analysis, corroboration scoring, and historical accuracy tracking across published print editions.

Factors.Name: Document Corroboration Rate
Factors.Description: Percentage of source-provided documents independently verified through FOIA requests and archival cross-referencing

Factors.Name: Source Historical Accuracy
Factors.Description: Track record of prior tips and documents from the same source that led to published and unchallenged print articles

Factors.Name: Geographic Proximity to Subject
Factors.Description: Source proximity to the institutions or individuals under investigation, weighted by access plausibility

Factors.Name: Financial Motivation Assessment
Factors.Description: Evaluation of potential financial incentives or conflicts of interest that may compromise source credibility

Factors.Name: Relationship Network Density
Factors.Description: Density of known relationships between source and investigation subjects, indicating potential bias or access

Models.Name: Publication Risk Model
Models.Description: Assesses legal, ethical, and reputational risks associated with publishing investigative print articles. Evaluates defamation exposure, source protection obligations, public interest justification, and editorial standards compliance for long-form print exposes.

Factors.Name: Defamation Liability Score
Factors.Description: Probability of successful defamation action based on evidence strength, public interest defence, and subject profile

Factors.Name: Source Protection Exposure
Factors.Description: Risk of source identification through published content analysis, even after redaction and anonymisation

Factors.Name: Print Distribution Reach Impact
Factors.Description: Calculated impact based on print circulation figures, regional distribution, and estimated readership multiplier

Factors.Name: Financial Impact on Subject
Factors.Description: Estimated financial consequences for investigation subjects following print publication, including share price and contract effects

Factors.Name: Transaction Pattern Anomaly
Factors.Description: Detection of irregular financial transactions linked to investigation subjects identified through archival research

Models.Name: Investigation Security Model
Models.Description: Monitors operational security risks during active print newspaper investigations. Tracks potential information leaks, surveillance indicators, digital and physical security threats to journalists and sources, and chain-of-custody integrity for evidentiary documents.

Factors.Name: Information Leak Probability
Factors.Description: Likelihood of premature disclosure of investigation details before print publication date based on access patterns

Factors.Name: Journalist Physical Security Threat
Factors.Description: Assessment of physical threat level to investigative journalists based on subject profiles and regional risk factors

Factors.Name: Document Chain-of-Custody Integrity
Factors.Description: Verification score for evidentiary document handling from source acquisition through print publication

Factors.Name: Financial Trail Concealment
Factors.Description: Degree to which investigation subjects have attempted to obscure financial records discovered through FOIA requests

Factors.Name: Network Infiltration Risk
Factors.Description: Probability that investigation subjects or associates have penetrated the journalist-source relationship network
